name: Tamatar Bhat (Tomato Pilaf)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the onion and cook, stirring until light brown (about 7 minutes).
2. Add the coriander and let sizzle for 25 to 30 seconds.
3. Add the tomatoes and water. Mix well and cook over low heat, covered, for 15 minutes or until reduced to a pulpy sauce.
4. Add salt, pepper, rice, and water. Gently mix to fold the rice into the sauce.
5. Stir in butter and coat the rice evenly. Cover tightly and steam the rice over low heat for 15 to 20 minutes, or until the sauce is absorbed into the rice.
6. Fluff the rice with a fork and serve with fried potatoes or grated/sliced daikon radish if desired.
Fried Potatoes:
4 tablespoons Light Vegetable Oil
1 pound Potatoes, diced into 1/4-inch pieces with skin
Heat the oil in a large frying pan over medium heat until very hot. Add the potatoes and cook, turning and tossing, until browned and cooked (20-25 minutes). Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
